summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special Zoning Workshop with the Zoning Commission and Board of Zoning Appeals to discuss potential text amendments to the zoning code. Key discussions included administrative relief approval processes, minimum lot sizes for PUDs, the need for a land use plan before making major zoning changes, and various topics such as food trucks, corner lot standards, fencing, tiny homes, barndominiums, Airbnbs, solar panels, and temporary businesses. The group prioritized pursuing a future land use plan as a foundational step and agreed to tackle simpler code fixes in the meantime.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Agreed that the 10% administrative relief language would reference 'zoning inspector and zoning supervisor' instead of 'township administrator' to align with ORC requirements
Agreed to proceed with previously discussed text amendments through the Zoning Commission
Agreed that a future land use plan should be completed before making major zoning changes like lot sizes and rezoning
Motion to adjourn at 8:20 PM passed
action_items: Liz to finalize language on administrative relief text amendments incorporating 'zoning supervisor' terminology
Liz to review residential, industrial/business, and mixed-use PUD standards and provide expert opinion for next meeting
Liz to develop a proposal/scope for a future land use plan process, potentially starting with goals/objectives and future land use map
Liz to consult with Alyssa on common complaint items to help prioritize zoning code fixes
Liz to draft proposed language for corner lot standards and fencing regulations
Liz to draft food truck and temporary business regulations
Liz to look into parking lot requirements for businesses and nonconforming use issues
Julia to research whether statutory townships have authority to regulate Airbnbs/short-term rentals
Liz to check with Alyssa on businesses approved during change of command that have not completed agreed-upon requirements
Trustees/staff to attend solar panel meeting at Bethel Township on Thursday
key_amounts: Current minimum PUD size is 10 acres
Current R1 minimum lot size is 4,000 square feet, discussion of increasing to 2 acres
Current minimum house size is 1,400 square feet in R1
PUD minimum lot size is 5,000 square feet
8,000 square foot lots require rear alley loaded garages in PUDs
Residential PUD with 35% open space allows up to 6 units per acre
Residential PUD with 50% open space allows up to 8 units per acre
Mixed-use PUD requires minimum 25% open space
Business PUD cannot exceed 70% paved/hard surface
Miami Township land use plan references 5 acres for large lot/low density residential
Administrative relief threshold is less than 10%
Front yard fence height limit discussed at 4 feet versus 8 feet
notable_topics: Administrative relief approval process and ORC compliance for zoning inspector authority
Minimum lot sizes and PUD standards for residential developments
Need for a comprehensive land use plan before major zoning changes
Solar panel farm regulations and upcoming multi-township meeting
Food truck and temporary business regulations
Corner lot standards and fencing/privacy fence requirements
Airbnb/short-term rental regulation authority for statutory townships
Tiny homes, barndominiums, and container homes
Small livestock and chicken regulations under ORC agricultural definitions
Architectural standards for planned unit developments
Potential creation of separate agricultural and residential zoning districts
EV charging stations, breweries/distilleries, and artisan industrial uses
attendees: David Seid (Zoning Commission member, 2231 State Route 132)
notes: This was a joint workshop with the Zoning Commission and Board of Zoning Appeals members present. The township previously started a land use plan project around 2020 that was interrupted, possibly by COVID and workload issues. A planning consultant had previously provided a proposed approach and scope. The Woodland Streams subdivision was referenced as a court-ordered affordable housing development that resulted from a lawsuit against prior trustees. Miami Township's recently adopted land use plan was referenced as a comparison model. Julia mentioned researching the ORC agricultural definition back to the 1960s and referenced a recent Union Township case involving livestock on small lots. Alyssa (zoning inspector) was not present but was referenced multiple times. A new zoning department employee hire is planned.
title: BOT Special Meeting 4.18.23
